He's previously been dubbed the 'hidden royal' - having missed occasions with his wider family in the past while concentrating on his schooling. But on Sunday, the Earl of Wessex proved he's firmly in the royal fold. James Mountbatten-Windsor, 18, was handed a more prominent role at the Royal Family's traditional Easter service, beaming alongside the Prince and Princess of Wales during his rare outing. Prince Edward and Sophie's son walked closely behind William, Kate and their three children, Prince George, 12, Princess Charlotte, ten, and Prince Louis, seven, as they arrived with other royals for the service at St George's Chapel in Windsor Castle. Joining his father, the Duke of Edinburgh, James was captured joyfully chatting with the Princess of Wales outside the chapel before entering the Easter Matins service led by the King and Queen.

  • 2 videos

But the whole idea is to erase this country's wonderful creative history. To replace our cultures and traditions with others. And eventually to replace our ancient native peoples too. No other country in the world would allow this destruction of our history and ways of life. And when assisted by her own last treacherous governments. Mostly philistines, who don't know their English literature. And, they have already started questioning Shakespeare's works. Video captured the moment a hooded thief uprooted a freshly planted hyacinth from a couple's flowerbed - before depositing it in her bag for life. In bizarre footage, the woman, who appears to be in her 60s, can be seen strolling along the driveway of the home of Dennis and Svitlana Jacobsen. She then swiftly pulls the flower out of the earth - taking a clump of roots with it. The thief carefully places the hyacinth plant in her large blue carrier bag before walking across the road where she eyes up another flowerbed, seemingly in her sights. Mr and Mrs Jacobsen had only planted the flower a few weeks earlier.
  • 29 comments
  • 1 video
  • share Hooded thief pulls freshly-planted hyacinth from a couple's flowerbed

Two young siblings from Colombia died after getting trapped in a fridge-freezer while they played a game of hide-and-seek. Saori Guevara Tiller, eight, and her five-year-old brother Darien, were rushed to the hospital after their parents discovered them inside the home appliance at around 7pm on Saturday, after returning from a 20-minute shopping trip. Doctors attempted to revive the children, but they tragically died. Police have confirmed they have now launched an investigation.
